goal: Build and publish Scout docs to onhyper.io

steps.name: Build Static Export
steps.result: Build completed successfully. 17 static pages generated in out/

steps.name: Fix routing issue on onhyper.io
steps.description: Investigate why routing/content not working on onhyper.io - possible pushState/client-side routing issue with static export
investigation: Check if Next.js static export uses pushState for navigation
Verify if onhyper.io supports SPA routing or needs trailingSlash config
Check if 404.html fallback is needed for client-side routes
Test direct URL access vs navigation from index
Consider adding trailingSlash: true to next.config.mjs for static hosts
steps.result: Added trailingSlash: true to next.config.mjs. Needs rebuild and redeploy.

steps.name: Document Agents & Drive functionality
steps.description: Review Scout OS changelog and plan documentation for new Agents and Drive features
subtasks: Review Scout OS changelog for recent agents/drive updates
Research how Agents work in Scout OS
Plan documentation structure for Agents section
Plan documentation structure for Drive section
Identify key concepts, APIs, and examples to document
sources: Scout OS product/app for hands-on testing

steps.name: Remove blocks and workflows from docs
steps.description: Remove blocks/ and workflows/ content directories from the documentation - focus on current Scout OS features
subtasks: Update content/_meta.js to remove navigation entries

tasks.name: Reorganize navigation - move workflows to bottom
tasks.description: Move the workflows section to the bottom of navigation or merge /workflows/agent-blocks into /agents/. Create _meta.json files to control navigation order.
tasks.result: Reordered navigation in content/_meta.js (Getting Started, Agents, Collections, Drive, Skills, Workflows, About). Moved agent-blocks.mdx from workflows/ to agents/ and updated both _meta.js files. Removed old file. Dev server tested successfully, build completed with 25 pages.

tasks.name: Audit all URLs in documentation
tasks.description: Verify studio.scoutos.com and api.scoutos.com endpoints are correct. Check all example URLs and API endpoints throughout the docs.

result.summary: CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND: 2 major domains returning 404 errors

studio.scoutos.com.note: BROKEN - Returns 404, hosted on Vercel with Clerk auth

api.scoutos.com: content/drive/api-reference.mdx - multiple endpoints (upload, download, folders, list)

recommendations: URGENT: Verify if studio.scoutos.com domain is correctly configured - currently returns 404
URGENT: Verify if api.scoutos.com domain is correctly configured - currently returns 404
Consider using working domains (e.g., app.scoutos.com?) or fixing DNS/routing for these subdomains
Update all API examples once correct API domain is confirmed
Email addresses (info@, support@) should be verified as working

tasks.name: Audit integration references
tasks.description: Verify all mentioned integrations exist: Fireflies, Slack, Google Workspace, Notion, CRMs, databases. Remove or update references to unavailable integrations.

result.summary: Integration audit completed. Most integrations are verified as available, with one notable exception.

integrations.name: Fireflies
integrations.context: Mentioned as workflow trigger: 'Fireflies meeting ends'
integrations.recommendation: REMOVE or mark as 'coming soon'. No official Scout integration exists. This appears aspirational or planned but not implemented.

integrations.name: Slack
integrations.context: Used for alerts, notifications, and AI chatbot integration
integrations.notes: Beta status correctly noted in docs. Integration confirmed at scoutos.com/integrations/slack

integrations.name: Google Workspace/Drive/Calendar/Sheets
integrations.context: Sync source with archetype ID: com.google.drive
integrations.notes: Multiple Google services integrated. Well documented.

integrations.name: Notion
integrations.context: Sync source with archetype ID: com.notion.notion. MCP now available.
integrations.notes: Strong integration confirmed with official docs

integrations.name: LinkedIn
integrations.context: Listed as '(optional)' in Sales Research Agent template
integrations.recommendation: Clarify if this requires custom OAuth setup or custom skill

integrations.name: Exa.ai
integrations.context: Third-party web search API, listed as optional
integrations.notes: Not a native integration - custom skill/api connection

recommendations: CRITICAL: Remove or qualify Fireflies references - integration does not exist
Clarify LinkedIn and Exa.ai as optional custom integrations rather than built-in
Consider adding 'Available Integrations' reference table to agents/getting-started.mdx
Sync sources (Notion, Google Drive, Microsoft 365) are well documented in skills/available-skills.mdx

tasks.name: Clarify Skills documentation
tasks.description: Determine if Skills is a Scout feature or OpenClaw-specific. Decide whether it should be public docs or internal. Update branding consistency.
tasks.result:
**Determination: Skills is a SCOUT feature, not OpenClaw-specific.**

**Issues Found (2 locations with incorrect branding):**

1. `/content/skills/overview.mdx` line ~56: "Skills can be installed using the OpenClaw CLI"
2. `/content/skills/creating-skills.mdx` line ~84: "# If using OpenClaw" section header

**Evidence that Skills is a Scout feature:**
- Package name is "scout-docs2" for Scout documentation
- Deployed to scout-docs.onhyper.io
- All other branding says "Scout Skills", "Scout OS", "Scout API"
- Available skills reference scoutos.com and Scout Dashboard
- Installation command in available-skills.mdx is `npx skills add scoutos/scout-skills` (no mention of OpenClaw)

**Recommended Changes:**
1. In `overview.mdx`: Change "Skills can be installed using the OpenClaw CLI" to "Skills can be installed using the Scout CLI" or "Skills can be installed using npx skills add"
2. In `creating-skills.mdx`: Change section header "# If using OpenClaw" to "# Installing Skills" or similar generic header
3. The skills documentation is correctly placed in public Scout docs - no relocation needed

tasks.name: Update SDK examples and package names
tasks.description: Verify 'scoutos' Python package name is correct. Update all code examples to use current SDK names and import paths.
tasks.result:
✅ ALL SDK PACKAGE NAMES ARE CORRECT. No changes needed.

**Python SDK (PyPI):**
- Package: scoutos ✓
- Install: pip install scoutos
- Import: from scoutos import Scout ✓

**TypeScript SDK (npm):**
- Package: scoutos ✓
- Install: npm i scoutos
- Import: import { ScoutClient } from "scoutos" ✓

**Files verified (18 SDK imports found):**
- content/drive/overview.mdx
- content/drive/api-reference.mdx
- content/collections/creating-collections.mdx
- content/collections/overview.mdx
- content/collections/querying-data.mdx

All imports match the official SDK documentation on PyPI and npm.
